findings.evidence: `nl -ba .../AmbientIsotopy.lean` lines 101-108 show `of_ambientIsotopy` takes `Φ.final.comp f.toContinuousMap = g.toContinuousMap`, while line 107 names the reversed equation theorem `of_eq_final` although line 108 is `g.toContinuousMap = Φ.final.comp f.toContinuousMap`.
findings.fix: Rename it to a conclusion-oriented endpoint name such as `of_eq_final_comp` or `of_eq_final_comp_toContinuousMap`, matching the actual `Φ.final.comp f.toContinuousMap` expression.
findings.issue: `SmoothEmbedding.AmbientIsotopic.of_eq_final` understates the endpoint equation: the hypothesis is `g.toContinuousMap = Φ.final.comp f.toContinuousMap`, not equality to `Φ.final` itself.

summary: One introduced constructor name is misleading: it names only `final` but the hypothesis is equality with the postcomposition `Φ.final.comp f.toContinuousMap`. The rest of the introduced names follow the adjacent ambient-isotopy API.
